text:
*"Don't Read this unless you saw the first Candyman from 1992,"*  

(Or actually, any of the other movies)


In case anyone is interested, the main character's name is Anthony. And he was the Baby that Candyman kidnapped in the first movie. He threatened a woman named Helen (the reincarnation of his lover) who was researching him as part of her thesis, with killing Baby Anthony if she didn't go to him. The Candyman intended for Helen and Anthony to die in a bonfire and join him as part of a weird ghost family. Helen died but not before she took Anthony out and saved him. 

In the end, Helen turns into a Vengeance Ghost that uses a hook similar to Candyman and will appear in a mirror if summoned. She ends up having her own mural painted within Candyman's Urban Temple and now she's part of the Candyman Folklore of Cabrini-Green. In the last Candyman movie we saw that the reincarnation of his granddaughter defeated him in the third movie. Her Mom (Candyman's Daughter reborn) who was in the second movie, told her that to defeat the Candyman, she needs to kill his legend. He seems to feed on raw belief. 

But whenever he doesn't have enough fear or belief to feed off of, he had to kill. So she blamed all the murders on a crooked Cop who tried to kill her in the end of the movie. Making people stop believing in the Candyman. It seems Anthony has rekindled the Candyman's soul by getting people hyped about him again. And stupidly getting people to do the ritual. 

Helen's Ghost is going to be in the movie as well I have heard. I thought it was awesome that they put Anthony as the main character. And the setting back in Cabrini-Green. Though wikipedia says it will be a heavily gentrified Cabrini-Green.

text:
The question of why people like horror has been well-discussed, and tend to hinge on the idea that some people enjoy the rush of adrenaline in a protected atmosphere or the sharp contrast between terror and then relief.

But for every person with a season pass to ScareHouse, there are probably five who couldn't be dragged, even kicking and screaming, into a horror movie. The more interesting question is, then: what predicts which camp you find yourself in?

At the University of Cambridge, psychologist Peter Rentfrow and some co-authors performed an elegant study in which their participants reported on their preferred genres of entertainment—books, movies, music, and television shows—and also took personality tests1. The first thing Rentfrow and his team did was see how different genres clustered together. For instance, did fans of horror movies usually also like spy novels? Did sitcom fans like pop music?

The results of this analysis were seriously fascinating. People's preferences clustered into easily identified themes. One of the clusters they called "Communal"; here we find your friends who love "Modern Family," Taylor Swift, and things starring Hugh Grant—entertainment that is "lighthearted, uncomplicated, and popular." Other clusters included "Aesthetic" (poetry, blues, foreign flicks), "Thrilling" ("Breaking Bad," spy movies), and "Cerebral" (mostly non-fiction).

The bin we're most interested in, of course, is the one they labeled simply "Dark." These people like punk and heavy metal, horror, cult entertainment, and the erotic; entertainment that is "characterized by intensity, edginess, and hedonism."

So what are these Dark people like, personality wise? In contrast to those high on the Communal group, who tended to be warm, pleasant, and friendly (though not terribly creative), Dark cluster people tended to score low on measures of duty, cautiousness, and cooperation, but high on ingenuity, provocativeness, and self-disclosure. They also tended to be young, male, and educated. The authors summed up people who like horror as "defiant, reckless, and immodest."

text:
⮚ Candyman (2020) ⮘
 WATCH HERE ► ► candyman-2020-faymovie.blogspot.com
在整個人類歷史上，強者，富人和具有狡猾特質的人捕食部落，氏族，城鎮，城市和鄉村中的弱者，無`'守和貧窮成員。然而，人類的生存意願迫使那些被拒絕，被剝奪或摧毀的基本需求的人們找到了一種生活方式，並繼續將其DNA融入不斷發展的人類社會。
說到食物，不要以為那些被拒絕的人只吃垃圾。相反，他們學會了在被忽視的肉類和蔬菜中尋找營養。他們學會了清潔，切塊，調味和慢燉慢燉的野菜和肉類，在食品市場上被忽略的部分家用蔬菜和肉類，並且學會了使用芳香的木煙（如山核桃，山核桃和豆科灌木）來調味食物煮的時候
author: @mayachanel7798

text: ITS NOT A REMAKE! The film will serve as a "spiritual sequel", taking place back in the new gentrified Cabrini Green where the old housing projects development once stood in Chicago. Anthony McCoy is the baby Helen saved from the fire in the original film. His mother "Anne-Marie McCoy" (Vanessa Williams) reprises her role in this. From the trailer, it reminds me of "A Nightmare on Elm Street 2:Freddys Revenge". Where 'Freddy 'used Jessies body as a conduit to kill for him

text: 6 years ago , my friend ( let's call her Amy) said the word " Candy Man" 5 times at school because she was challenged by a someone .(We done lot of stupid things) The next morning , my classmates started to feel strange vibe around us but we ignored it . On the second day , there was one of my friend(let's call Cassy)suddenly cried when the teacher was teaching. We all looking at her and the teacher even asked her whether she's okay or not . She kept repeating the same phrase which is " He's here !".  I had to admit , I got excited and scared but idk why(lmao). The next day ,she was absent but there were some of my classmates got scratched and I got it too . I still remembered,  my friend and I were very scared,we even prayed to God . I'm going to skip some of the part because the story is too long. So we found this octopus doll at our school and Cassie started to cry and act like a psycho.  My friend Amy was really quiet tha lately . We found some blood and some of my classmate even started to seeing things . We were very scared to go to school . The teacher knew what happened but she didn't want to tell us . After this paranormal thingy happened multiple of time , our principal called the priest and he doing his things .( ya'll know what I referred) After that , things were getting better than before but unfortunately my friend Amy has serious mental health right now which is is depression. After that things happened,  she kept thinking about suicide thought (she's better now at least) I didn't tell y'all her part of the story because it will be sooo long but her part was the scariest.  Y'all can say my story is fake or nah but this was based on true story and I experienced it . Idk whether it because of the "Candy Man" but yeah . ( sorry for my bad grammar,  English isn't my first language)

text:
I never understood, nor do I think I will ever understand Hollywood's obsession with the supernatural: whether they be fictional or based on actual events.

What I mean is, in all these scary death-defying or near-death experience where the protaganist(s) are faced with a supernatural entity, they either die horrible deaths or they think they won the battle against the spirit(s) but the movie still ends in a horror cliffhanger. Fuck.

Why don't they just get on their knees and pray for strenght and protection against those evil spirits? Why won't they call out on Jesus to save them? Better to call on Jesus and let Him save you than die and suffer in eternal bloody damnation.

text:
I called it...I UNINTENUALLY CALLED IT...Idk if I should be happy or angry...How about both...Wanna know how I called this. okay, a month ago Feb 26th, I was complaining about how they were making remakes of classic horror movies to my father and how people are just making remakes for the cash grab. Then I said in my own freaking words "What's next Candyman?, The Craft?, Cursed?, Dead End?, Ginger Snap?, Orphan?!"


Then the next day a Candyman trailer comes out...and I'm seeing it now...On March 3rd ...What...The Heck... (oh no now I realized I gave them more Idea O_O'/// *CENSOR* !!! And I'm not saying cancel it because it belongs to the company. All I'm gonna say is that remaking movies over and over again gets annoying like why not just call it Candyman 2 or 3, hell even 4 is better instead of Candyman 1. Sure it's been over a decade or two since Candyman was created BUT that doesn't mean you can't come up with a new name for it.) So I'm not gonna Like it, but I'm not gonna Dislike it either there would be no point...cuz this is literally Candyman 4.

text:
Most horror fans are aware that if you say look into a mirror and say Candyman 5 times the angry spirit of Tony Todd will appear and murder you. What many do not know is that if you say Candyman a sixth time, the spirit of Sammy Davis Jr. will appear, and if you say it a seventh time, the spirit of Gene Wilder will appear and perform a duet.

This is why the Candyman is so angry. He's been waiting for the floor show and nobody has ever bothered to get it started.

text:
It was the starting of year 2020, people going about their lives as usual,  still watching  trailers for movies of fake monsters and myths on Youtube when it all started. Everyone had an Idea something was going on on the other side of the globe, something meme worthy..something which sounded like a beer brand  they had got high on, but it was not something which had their attention as much it was needed to prevent the dystopian future that was about to come.

Oblivious to the extent of damage this virus was about to inflict on the world and what it was about to do to them, people were still chilling getting their goosebumps watching fictional  monsters like Candyman, little did they know the people they had known their whole lives where about to be mutated into something far worse then Candyman or Freddy Kruger, in fact they where about to be turned into something that would even give The Predator  and Pennywise endless nightmares if they were real.



The feast was about to begin and there was no where to hide, everyone was invited for dinner and everyone was on the menu.

- time traveler

text: This looks underwhelming. In all fairness, it is very hard to compete with the original Candyman. The original movie was very poetic and classy. The fear relied on the unseen. The acting was amazing in the first Candyman. Along with the pacing and dialogues. The main actor's voice was deep and hypnotic. The main actress had the bluest eyes and she looked gorgeous. Watching the trailer of this new version, it's giving me all types of boring "Scream" and Teenager nonsense. Not excited at all. The pacing of the dialogues seems very fast, like most movies these days. The story seems to be going all over the place without one main idea. It seems to be very graphic too, which prevents viewer's imagination from drifting away and filling the blanks. I can tell this film will be lame as hell!  Not excited at all !!

text:
If you pause to see the frames during a few of the scenes it seems as if Candyman is actually several people including the main character in the trailer. 
1. At the gallery killing scene, Candyman is very short with longer hair under a cap and a nicer coat. Unless that was a real bad stunt coordinator.
2. When the main character sees Candyman in the mirror and puts up his hand to defend himself, Candyman is holding the same pose. Also, his wounded hand is the same as Candyman’s hook hand (amputated hand).
Maybe in this version, Candyman is a spirit that inhabits individuals to do his bidding rather than just a being that comes out of the mirror and kills you. Which could be amazing if done right.

text:
Following a three-day public hearing in December 2018, the second public hearing of the Independent People's Tribunal/China Tribunal was held in London on April 6-7, 2019.

Twenty-three witnesses attended the tribunal, including researchers, medical professionals, journalists, and survivors of the atrocities committed by the Chinese communist regime from around the world. They gave testimonies before the tribunal regarding allegations of state-sanctioned organ harvesting targeting persecuted groups in China. Chaired by Sir Geoffrey Nice, QC, the panel consisted of seven experts tasked with conducting the world’s first-ever independent analysis of forced organ extraction in China.


The second public hearing of the Independent People's Tribunal/China Tribunal was held in London.

The witnesses came from the U.S., Canada, France, Japan, Korea, and Turkey. Six Falun Gong practitioners and three Uyghurs recounted their experiences of being abused while imprisoned, and undergoing blood tests and CT scans. Medical experts, investigators, and investigative journalists gave testimonies of their findings.

The tribunal is due to release its final judgment on June 17, regarding whether the international crimes of forced organ harvesting in China have been committed, and if they are still being committed.

Falun Gong Practitioners Present Evidence of CCP's Persecution

Falun Gong practitioner Yu Ming presents pictures and videos of the Chinese Communist Party's (CCP) persecution to the tribunal via video link. (Picture source: endtransplantabuse.org)

text:
Why do we need Jesus? 

We are in a fallen world full of wickedness, pain, and suffering,  separated from God by sin. So what must be done to restore fellowship with God? First we must be genuine about our own sins. Look at the 10 commandments and see how you stack up, it is a mirror showing us our sinful nature. We have all broken the moral law and deserve his wrath and judgement. 

The Gospel: 

At the same time the Father is so rich in mercy and loves us so much that he sent his Son in the flesh 2000 years ago, who lived a sinless life on our behalf, was gruesomely tortured and mocked by his own creation, died a horrific death on the cross in place of US filthy sinners, and rose from the dead 3 days later proving he is the Great I Am. He paid the fine for our death sentence on the cross thus satisfying God's justice, setting us free from sin and the Law. Once we realize our sinful nature,  we must repent for our sins, and put our full trust in the Lord and Savior Jesus Christ like you'd put faith in a parachute. Do this and you will be born again. He will  instantly grant you the Holy Spirit which will live in you, eternal life in a united heaven and earth, a new heart that loves righteousness, and relationship with God will be made right, not by our good works or observance of the law, but by his AMAZING GRACE and mercy. That is His promise. Humbly ask him to reveal himself to you. Think about this. If you have a Bible I invite you to read the book of John. Jesus loves you, I love you, peace.

Dear, Universal pictures, 
I loved the first movie. I think this will be an okay reboot/sequel.

To be honest, I didn't really feel the fear element that the first film had. One reason is that the music in the trailer wasn't ominous or didn't evoke mystery. text:
I saw this movie by accident as a kid.
Bragged about it in daycare.
Got locked in the bathroom by peers.
Couldn't leave until I said his name.
I still remember how horrifying it was.
I never told anyone.
This was after my mother was attacked.
Intruder came and beat her with a bat.
Took nothing except my sanity.
I've had next to no one to talk to.
Even mom avoided conversation.
After being in a coma for 3 years.
Everytime I thought of this movie it was like a floodgate had opened.
Here we go again.

text:
I can totally see how it’s addressing toxic fandom. This dude seems a little obsessed with Candyman.

My guess, C-man is using this to his advantage and is trying to pull a Freddy’s Revenge and take over his body. 

Apparently leaks are saying that Tony Todd’s Candyman isn’t in the movie too much.

Now, I don’t really believe leaks. But, I do think that he’s going to be used very sparingly and it’s going to focus more on the protagonist possibly turning into the new Candyman. And then, there’s going to be a lot more of Tony Todd’s Candyman in the final battle. 

If there’s a sequel, which there undoubtedly will be with the state of Hollywood, there will be a bit more of Tony’s Candyman. 

But that’s all just my theory. I can’t wait to see this movie! It looks awesome!

text:
Great..Another 'retelling' of a horror classic. Loaded with CGI - CGI 
bees, invisible murderers killing with CGI, loads of young, hip 
characters including some SJW types. The original was great because the 
story was simple but also deep behind the surface..it wasn't generic. It
 didn't make a big deal of dealing with black culture..it just existed 
in it without prejudice. It had an nontraditional classic phillip glass 
soundtrack. This is going to be a run of the mill netflix type generic 
horror with borrowed characters, generic soundtrack, same slick look as 
every other mainstream horror of the last 5 years, current 'woke' 
subtext which will just reduce it to virtue signaling. To me this seems 
so uninteresting..and yes I know its Jordan Peele, but 'Us' was also 
overrated...just my opinion, but I'm honestly getting sick of all these 
classic remakes, which are so 'current' but are forgotten forever after 2
 years. Poltergeist, Nightmare on Elm Street, Friday 13th, The new 
Halloweens, The Grudge, Child's Play...do any of these films understand 
what made the originals so great?
author: @AutoSMR

text: Hmmm I've only seen the first Candyman, and I'm not at all familiar with 2 or 3, but I kinda liked that the original had a female lead. I'll still see this, but now it's like... about men 😕 man v. man. As if we don't see that dynamic all the time. I know I'm being a buzzkill lol but I just liked the original so much and I want the remake to do it justice. We shall see. Also, her being white played a part in the story as well, actually put her at a higher risk stepping into an urban community where she "didn't belong" ... so let's see if this man is made out to be an outcast when he dives into his research of the legends. Another thing about her being the one who was doing the investigation was that it drove home the sort of BLM point that the tall tale only existed because people never legitimately looked into the murders of people who are poor or minorities. Like that quote from Nightcrawler: "We find our viewers are more interested in urban crime creeping into the suburbs. What that means is a victim or victims, preferably well-off and/or white, injured at the hands of the poor, or a minority." And in Candyman, that is why nobody really knew or cared about him until a white lady came sniffing around... and people hated her for basically appropriating their culture, calling him a myth when they KNEW he existed. Lots of different tensions playing off each other in the original film. ANYWAY END RANT I'm excited for the remake and can't wait to see what they do with any new changes!

text: The story of "Candyman" is one of racism and love. He fell in love with a woman and fathered her child. Because of a forbidden love, he was tortured, humiliated and murdered. I love the original "Candyman". The beautiful mysterious score and the great injustice helped to make this character unique. I don't know if this apparent reboot/remake can recreate the nobility and seductive qualities of "Candyman" the way Tony Todd did. I've seen remakes from my childhood distorted and watered down because of a lack of love and originality on Hollywood's part.

text: The original Candyman was stupid scary. Was basically the only horror movie that ever scared me and it had zero comedy in it, a really serious movie. The Candyman origin and mythology was interesting and I thought it was ironic that he was an artist who used his hand to paint beautiful portraits, but when his hand was sawed off and was replaced with a hook while in his ghoul form, it was used to kill and shed blood instead of creating art. And he kills you in a really horrible, beyond painful way. I guess I'll have to visit that scary place again if I ever see this new movie, but Tony Todd will always be the original Candyman. Is he returning as Candyman, that would be so tight. I like how the girl from the first movie is in it, her baby must be an adult now.
